svg.text: YORUBA
GRAMMAR & STRUCTURE
YORUBA WORD ORDER: SVO
Mo jẹ iyàn. "I eat pounded yam." (SVO)
Modifiers follow nouns: àdàba dudú "pigeon black" = "black pigeon"
No morphological case; word order + particles signal grammatical role
YORUBA VERB SERIALIZATION
Serial verbs: multiple verbs in sequence without conjunction:
Mo mú owó ra îwé. "I take money buy book."
"I bought a book with money." (serial: take-money-buy-book)
Common across West Africa; unusual in world languages
YORUBA NOUNS & TONES IN GRAMMAR
No gender • No case • No number agreement
Plural: often indicated by context or reduplication
Tone changes meaning but also grammar: High = noun, Low = verb
TWI GRAMMAR · SECONDARY LANGUAGE
TWI WORD ORDER: SVO
Twi is also SVO (same as Yoruba)
ɔberima no dɛi fufuo. "The man eats fufu." (SVO)
Modifiers follow head: same pattern as Yoruba
TWI VERB SERIALIZATION
Twi ALSO uses serial verbs (West African areal feature!)
ɔ fa sika no kɔ. "Take money go." = "He went with money."
Parallel structure to Yoruba serial verb construction
TWI NOUN CLASSES (AKAN)
Akan has noun classes (unlike Yoruba which has none):
Singular: ɔ-baa "woman" • Plural: a-baa "women" (prefix changes)
About 9 noun classes with different plural prefixes
SHARED WEST AFRICAN GRAMMAR
Both: SVO • both: serial verbs • both: tone-based grammar
Both: no case • both: modifiers after noun
Yoruba: no noun classes • Twi: noun class system
Serial verbs: same construction in both languages
PLATE 30c ALT · YORUBA / TWI · GRAMMAR
